title: Table split (complex schema restructuring)
description: The user_addresses table is replaced by two purpose-specific tables: billing_addresses and shipping_addresses. The original table is dropped, removing the address_type discriminator column in favor of structural separation. Any service that queries or writes to user_addresses will break immediately. The split also loses the address_type column entirely since the new tables encode address type implicitly via table name. A phased migration plan is required: backfill new tables from old data, migrate readers, migrate writers, then drop the old table.

schema_changes.reason: Dropping an existing table breaks all readers and writers that reference it. Any ORM mapping, raw SQL, or migration referencing user_addresses will fail.
Adding a new table is a purely additive, safe change. No existing consumers are affected.
Adding a new table is a purely additive, safe change. No existing consumers are affected.
